items.title: NT government announces details of review into Kumanjayi Little Baby’s situation

children.content: The NT government has revealed the scope of a review into the territory's child protection system, and announced that it will be led by retired NSW police commissioner Karen Webb and long-standing territory public servant Greg Shanahan.

gallery.altText: Flowers on a fence with a note reading fly high baby girl

thumbnail.altText: Flowers on a fence with a note reading fly high baby girl

avatar.alt: An Australian-Egyptian woman with dark brown shoulder-length curly hair smiling at camera, in a green patterned top.

items.title: NT Police fraud squad seizes grenades, warheads in raids south of Darwin

children.content: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

image.altText: A collection of military ordnance.

items.title: Man on mobility scooter killed by car in Alice Springs

children.content: A 49-year-old man using a mobility scooter has died after being hit by a car on a pedestrian crossing in Alice Springs.

image.altText: NT Police car flashing lights

items.title: 'Something catastrophic is going to happen': NT mayor on car thefts spike

children.content: Katherine's mayor says residents feel less safe than they did a year ago and is calling on Northern Territory Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro to hold an "urgent" roundtable on crime in the Top End town.

gallery.altText: A woman looking over floodwaters with a concerned expression on her face.

thumbnail.altText: A woman looking over floodwaters with a concerned expression on her face.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price leads emotional tributes for Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price weeps through an impassioned tribute to her niece, Kumanjayi Little Baby, in federal parliament, amid a push to broaden the focus of an inquiry announced in the wake of her alleged murder.

image.altText: An Indigenous politician giving a speech inside red Senate chambers, she is crying as she holds a tissue to her face.

items.title: Australian company Heytesbury buys mega fleet of 11 live export ships

children.content: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

image.altText: A large grey live export ship docked at a port.

items.title: Legal Aid NT flags more cuts to services amid ongoing funding crisis

children.content: One of two legal bodies responsible for representing vulnerable Territorians in court is considering making further cuts, six months after its services were previously slashed.

gallery.altText: An empty court bench in front of the NT coat of arms

thumbnail.altText: An empty court bench in front of the NT coat of arms

items.title: Darwin company's $50 million plan to make diesel from gas condensate

children.content: Global Resource Recovery NT says production of the  first diesel made from Central Australian gas condensate will leave Darwin in July.

image.altText: A large industrial facility with stainless steel pipes and tanks with red earth visible in the background.

items.title: Matt Wright back with family after what he says was 'one hell of a fight'

children.content: Outback Wrangler star Matt Wright has served five months in a Darwin prison, revealing he was not released in time for the birth of his third child.

image.altText: A husband and wife with their three children, sitting on the veranda of a family home and smiling at the camera.

items.title: NT Police say pepper spraying Network 10 camera operator 'not inappropriate'

children.content: Northern Territory Police have reviewed an incident in which an officer pepper sprayed a Network 10 camera operator during rioting in Alice Springs last month, concluding his actions were "not inappropriate".

image.altText: A black-circle, around a coloured image of a police officer spraying a camera operator, photographer in foreground.

gallery.altText: A blurry photo of a white man, police uniform, spraying pepper spray at channel 10 camera operator who is holding stop hand out.

thumbnail.altText: A blurry photo of a white man, police uniform, spraying pepper spray at channel 10 camera operator who is holding stop hand out.

items.title: How Australians across generations feel about the budget

children.content: Australians across generations, from gen Z to baby boomers, weigh in on the "intergenerational equity" budget.

A man holding a coffee mug in a kitchen.
A older man sitting in his lounge room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds a coffee mug.

A older man sitting in his lounge room.

items.title: New report accuses Hamas of 'deliberately' using rape on October 7

children.content: A 300-page report from an Israeli non-profit organisation has detailed allegations of horrific sexual assault against victims of Hamas's October 7, 2023, attacks on Israel, accusing the group of "systematically and deliberately" using rape and sexual torture.

image.altText: A person with an Israeli flag draped on their shoulders stands in the middle of signs with peoples photos outside

items.title: Ivan Cleary to step away from Panthers after 2027 NRL season

children.content: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season. Follow live.

image.altText: Panthers coach Ivan Cleary reacts during a press conference, holding his head up with one hand

images.altText: Trump waves to reporters while he boards Air Force One on May 12.

articles.title: Australia insists support for UAE does not involve action against Iran

children.content: The Albanese government insists Australia is not involved in any "offensive action against Iran" amid reports the UAE, which had been supplied a Wedgetail aircraft, carried out military strikes against the country.

image.altText: A large, grey RAAF aircraft with a huge dorsal fin, in a blue sky.

children.content: US President Donald Trump says he does not need help from Beijing to end the war with Iran but says he will talk at length with Chinese President Xi Jinping when they hold a summit this week. Follow live.

items.title: Trump labels critical news reports on Iran 'virtual treason'

updates.title: Australia joins UK, French counterparts in meeting over Strait of Hormuz
Lebanese authorities estimate 380 killed in Israeli attacks since ceasefire began
Kuwait says four people affiliated with Iran's IRGC arrested trying to enter by sea

items.title: Iran war becomes a 'double-edged sword' for China as Trump visits

children.content: As US President Donald Trump prepares to meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing, some analysts say the war in Iran has significantly shifted the power dynamics in China's favour.

image.altText: Donald Trump's walks outside in strong wind, which blows his tie sideways.

items.title: One Nation's win a concern for minorities living in Farrer

children.content: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

image.altText: A man wearing a black hat looks at the camera

items.title: Eurovision pays musical homage to Austria-Australia confusion

children.content: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest included a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia, and sent 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

gallery.altText: a giant 'no kangaroos in austria' sign is projected behind dancers on a stage
dancers on stage wearing Austrian and Australian clothing

thumbnail.altText: a giant 'no kangaroos in austria' sign is projected behind dancers on a stage
dancers on stage wearing Austrian and Australian clothing

items.title: Coronial inquest into death of tasered 95yo Clare Nowland begins

children.content: Three years after the great-grandmother was tasered by a police officer in her aged care home, an inquest is expected to focus on dementia training for aged care staff and emergency services.

image.altText: an elderly woman looking at the camera

items.title: How Broadbeach became a blues mecca

children.content: As some festivals crumble, the Gold Coast's mayor says the 25-year-old Blues On Broadbeach event isn't going anywhere.

image.altText: Large crowd watches Blues on Broadbeach stage at night, lit by bright lights under cloudy sky.

items.title: The pianist fighting the MSO over right to bring whole self to stage

children.content: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, Australian man, 39, in a blazer and white shirt, concentrates as he plays piano in a concert hall.

items.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?

children.content: It's been 18 years of hosting Gruen for Wil Anderson, but the self-confessed advertising cynic says the topic is more relevant than ever.

image.altText: Wil Anderson in a suit smiling on a television set with a white mug in front of him.

items.title: Australian-born MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to knife British PM

children.content: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.

gallery.altText: A woman smiles at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles at the camera.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: 'Small comfort': Federal budget's $600m for Bondi terror attack response

children.content: The Executive Council of Australian Jewry has welcomed a $600 million federal budget funding commitment for the Jewish community, but says it is a "small comfort" for those who lost loved ones in the Bondi terror attack.

image.altText: Floral tributes on promenade at Bondi Beach

items.title: Solar eclipse to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere' in 2028

children.content: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

image.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky

items.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist advised to test for bloodborne viruses

children.content: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

image.altText: A person receiving dental treatment.

items.title: British paratroopers drop onto remote island after suspected hantavirus case

children.content: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

image.altText: A paratrooper with equipment strapped to his front jumps out of an airplane.

items.title: How fuel prices have impacted car sales

children.content: Electric vehicles now represent 16 per cent of new car sales in Australia, in a growing trend coinciding with record fuel prices.

gallery.altText: A line of vehicles in a car park

thumbnail.altText: A line of vehicles in a car park

items.title: Police confirm body found in remote nature reserve is triple murderer

children.content: NSW Police has confirmed the body of a man found beside a vehicle in the state’s central west was that of triple murderer Julian Ingram.

image.altText: Police tape in focus with a backdrop of grass and trees

items.title: Terror accused charged with child abuse, extremist material offences

children.content: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.

image.altText: A man smiles at a camera with a digger behind him

items.title: Voss says Carlton in a better place than when he took charge

children.content: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

gallery.altText: AFL coach walking off the field, looking down at his watch

thumbnail.altText: AFL coach walking off the field, looking down at his watch

items.title: Australia axes quick as Women's T20 World Cup squad is named

children.content: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

image.altText: Female fast bowler running through after delivering the ball

items.title: 'We are heartbroken': NBA in shock after death of Grizzlies forward

children.content: Memphis Grizzlies forward Clarke has died, the team, his agency and the NBA have confirmed.

image.altText: NBA player in street clothes walking through the corridors of an arena before a match

items.title: Farmers and environmentalists label federal budget 'savage' and 'tragic'

children.content: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.

image.altText: Rabbits on a rural property

items.title: E-scooter rider dies after being hit by car while crossing Perth highway

children.content: A man in his 40s is struck by a hatchback and killed while riding an e-scooter across Reid Highway in Perth's northern suburbs.

image.altText: A generic photograph of an unidentified WA Police officer wearing a high visibility police vest over a blue uniform.

items.title: Mornington Peninsula mourns as Sammy the seal believed dead

children.content: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.
